summary: A rectangular two-to-three story modern blocky building made primarily of pale sandstone-like walls with white corner/band accents, a flat red-tiled roof, a central raised tower/entrance with a small overhang and light on top, and a low white block perimeter fence on a dirt yard.

shape.overall_form: Mostly rectangular main volume with a central narrow tower/entrance projecting from the long axis; small courtyard/driveway area in front defined by a low fence.
shape.symmetry: approximate bilateral symmetry along the central tower axis

materials.name: quartz (or white concrete)
materials.role: corner trim / horizontal band / fence posts / edging

materials.name: slab_stone
materials.role: small roof cap on tower / walkway covering

elements: rows of small square windows in horizontal bands on each floor
central vertical tower/entrance with an overhang and a light on top
white low perimeter fence (single-block posts with gaps) surrounding a dirt yard
flat main roof edged with white trim and red/brick surface
white vertical buttresses/pillars along long facades
rebuild_hints: Build a rectangular footprint with two stacked stories of sandstone walls, add a horizontal white band between floors and white corner trims extending up to roofline.
Place small 1x1 or 1x2 glass windows in repeating horizontal rows (spacing ~3-4 blocks) on each floor.
Construct a central narrow tower projecting from the long face; top it with a small stone-slab overhang and a single sea-lantern or light block.
Use brick blocks for the flat roof surface and surround it with a 1-block high white trim/parapet.
Lay out a perimeter of white single-block posts spaced with one-block gaps on packed dirt ground to match the yard fence.
uncertainties: Exact block types: pale wall appears to be sandstone but could be chiseled/smooth variations; white trim may be quartz, white concrete, or wool.
Number of floors: visual cues show two main floors plus the small central tower and roof access; could be considered 3 levels depending on interior mezzanines.
Precise internal layout and door locations are not visible from provided exterior views.
Dimensions are approximate based on block-count estimation from images and may vary by a few blocks in each axis.
